name: Tools
summary: The Tools class contains methods to manipulate a collection of SketchUp tools. You access this collection by calling the Model.tools method.

methods.name: active_tool
methods.summary: The #active_tool method is used to obtain the active Ruby tool.
methods.remarks: The #active_tool method is used to obtain the active Ruby tool.

returns.doc: Returns the active Ruby tool, or `nil` otherwise.

methods.name: active_tool_id
methods.summary: The active_tool_id method is used to retrieve the active tool's id.
methods.remarks: The active_tool_id method is used to retrieve the active tool's id.

methods.name: active_tool_name
methods.summary: The active_tool_name method is used to retrieve the active tool's name.
methods.remarks: The active_tool_name method is used to retrieve the active tool's name.

methods.name: add_observer
methods.summary: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the current object.
methods.remarks: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the current object.

returns.doc: status - true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: model
methods.summary: The model method is used to get the model associated with this tools object.
methods.remarks: The model method is used to get the model associated with this tools object.

returns.doc: model - the Model object associated with this tools collection.

methods.name: pop_tool
methods.summary: The pop_tool method is used to pop the last pushed tool on the tool stack.
methods.remarks: The pop_tool method is used to pop the last pushed tool on the tool stack.

returns.doc: the last pushed Tool object, if it is a Ruby tool. If a native tool is active, selects the default native tool and returns nil.

methods.name: push_tool
methods.summary: The push_tool method is used to push (aka activate) a user-defined tool.
methods.remarks: The push_tool method is used to push (aka activate) a user-defined tool. See the Tool interface for details on creating your own SketchUp tool.

returns.doc: status - true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: remove_observer
methods.summary: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.
methods.remarks: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.
